svg.text: GREEK + ALBANIAN — RUNNING TEXT · PASSAGES
ALT DRAFT · SERIES 21D · REMEMBER FORWARD
— I. HERACLITUS — ON CHANGE (~500 BCE) —
Πάντα ρεί. (Panta rhei.)
"Everything flows." — Heraclitus of Ephesus. Oldest Greek philosophical fragment on change and time.
— II. PLATO — REPUBLIC (380 BCE) —
Γνώθι σεαυτόν. (Gnothi seauton.)
"Know thyself." — Inscribed at Delphi; foundational principle of Greek philosophy and Western ethics.
— III. GREEK BRIDGE PHRASE — REMEMBER FORWARD —
Αυτό έγινε για σένα, δωρεάν,
από ανθρώπους που θυμήθηκαν το μέλλον.
This was-made for you freely / by people who remembered the future.
"This was made for you, freely, by people who remembered the future." (Greek)
— GREEK NOTE — “FUTURE” AND “REMEMBER” —
μέλλον = future (from μέλλω, to be about to, to intend). θυμάμαι = to remember (lit. to call to mind).
δωρεάν = freely (adverb from δώρο = gift). Greek encodes free giving as gift-giving.
SECONDARY — ALBANIAN (SHQIP) — RUNNING TEXT
— I. NAIM FRASHËRI — ALBANIAN POET (1846–1900) —
Shqipëria më thirri, ë dashur dhe e nderëshme,
Albania me called beloved and honorable
"Albania called to me, beloved and honorable." — Frashëri, father of Albanian national literature.
— II. ALBANIAN PROVERB — ON MEMORY AND KNOWLEDGE —
Ai që nuk di të lexojë, është i verbër.
He who not can to-read is blind.
"He who cannot read is blind." — Albanian proverb. Context: Albanian literacy campaigns 1940s–1980s.
— III. ALBANIAN BRIDGE PHRASE — REMEMBER FORWARD —
Kjo është bërë për ju, falas,
nga njerëz që mbajtën mend të ardhmen.
This is made for you freely / by people who kept in-mind the future.
"This was made for you, freely, by people who kept the future in mind." (Albanian)
— ALBANIAN NOTE — “FUTURE” AND “REMEMBER” —
E ardhmja = the future (definite article e + ardhme from “arrive/come” — the future as what is arriving).
Mbaj mend = to remember (lit. to keep/hold + mind). A bodylike metaphor — memory as something held.
Falas = freely/for free. Suffixed definite article in “e ardhmja” shows the postposed article system.
— LANGUAGE BRIDGE — AN ANCIENT NEIGHBORHOOD —
Greek: 3500+ year written tradition. Ancestor of Western alphabets. ~13M speakers.
Albanian: ~7.5M speakers. An isolated Indo-European branch — no close relatives. Ancient substrate.
Shared region for millennia: Greeks and Illyrians (Albanian ancestors) were neighbors in antiquity.
Albanian preserves features of ancient Balkan languages that Greek absorbed and replaced.
PLATE 21D ALT · Greek / Albanian · RUNNING TEXT · CC BY-SA 4.0 · REMEMBERFORWARD.ORG
THIS IS A PATIENT MESSAGE. IT WAS MADE FOR YOU, FREELY, BY PEOPLE WHO REMEMBERED FORWARD.
BUY IT · BUILD IT · BURY IT · FOR SOMEONE YOU WILL NEVER MEET
